info.title: PMXT Hosted API
info.description: One API for every prediction market. Cross-venue search in under 10ms, a single unified schema, and the complete venue surface from reads to trades.

post.description: Load and cache all markets from the exchange into `this.markets` and `this.marketsBySlug`. Subsequent calls return the cached result without hitting the API again. This is the correct way to paginate or iterate over markets without drift. Because `fetchMarkets()` always hits the API, repeated calls with different `offset` values may return inconsistent results if the exchange reorders or adds markets between requests. Use `loadMarkets()` once to get a stable snapshot, then paginate over `Object.values(exchange.markets)` locally.

x-mint.content:
<Info>
**Faster with an API key** — With a PMXT API key this endpoint is served from an indexed catalog (~10 ms) instead of proxying to the venue (~500 ms). No code changes required — same request, same response, just faster. [Learn more](/concepts/catalog-vs-live).
</Info>

# API key optional — enables faster catalog-backed lookups

)
result = exchange.load_markets()

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset — number of results to skip

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by market status (default: 'active', 'inactive' and 'closed' are interchangeable)

parameters.name: outcomeId
parameters.description: Reverse lookup -- find market containing this outcome

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: Find markets belonging to an event

get.description: Fetch markets with optional filtering, search, or slug lookup. Always hits the exchange API — results reflect the live state at the time of the call.

get.description: Fetch markets with cursor-based pagination backed by a stable in-memory snapshot. On the first call (or when no cursor is supplied), fetches all markets once and caches them. Subsequent calls with a cursor returned from a previous call slice directly from the cached snapshot — no additional API calls are made. The snapshot is invalidated after `snapshotTTL` ms (configured via `ExchangeOptions` in the constructor). A request using a cursor from an expired snapshot throws `'Cursor has expired'`.

x-mint.content:
<Info>
**Faster with an API key** — With a PMXT API key this endpoint is served from an indexed catalog (~10 ms) instead of proxying to the venue (~500 ms). No code changes required — same request, same response, just faster. [Learn more](/concepts/catalog-vs-live).
</Info>

get.description: Paginated variant of {@link fetchEvents}. On the first call (no `cursor`), all events are fetched from the exchange and cached in an in-memory snapshot. A cursor is returned along with the first page. Subsequent calls with that cursor serve additional pages directly from the cached snapshot -- no additional API calls are made. The snapshot is invalidated after `snapshotTTL` ms (configured via `ExchangeOptions` in the constructor). A request using a cursor from an expired snapshot throws `'Cursor has expired'`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque venue pagination cursor, where supported.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset — number of results to skip

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by event status (default: 'active', 'inactive' and 'closed' are interchangeable)

parameters.name: series
parameters.description: Filter events by their parent series. Accepts the venue-native series id / ticker / slug (e.g. Kalshi `"KXATPMATCH"`, Polymarket `"wta"`). Passed through to the vendor where supported, otherwise applied to `sourceMetadata` after fetch.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Optional client-side filter applied after fetching

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by category. Each event belongs to a venue-assigned category such as "Sports", "Politics", "Crypto", "Bitcoin", "Soccer", "Economic Policy" (Polymarket) or "Sports", "Mentions" (Kalshi).

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter by tags. Returns events matching ANY of the provided tags. Tags are more specific than categories -- for example a "Politics" event might carry tags ["Politics", "Geopolitics", "Middle East", "Iran"]. Common tags include "Crypto", "Elections", "Fed Rates", "FIFA World Cup", "Trump".

get.description: Fetch events with optional keyword search. Events group related markets together (e.g., "Who will be Fed Chair?" contains multiple candidate markets).

get.description: Fetch the recurring series (fourth tier above Event -> Market -> Outcome) that this venue exposes. </Info>

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Outcome side: 'yes' or 'no'. Required for exchanges like Limitless where the API returns a single orderbook per market.

parameters.name: outcome
parameters.description: Outcome alias: 'yes' or 'no', or an outcome token ID. When set, the first argument is treated as a market ID and this value selects which outcome's order book to fetch. Accepts the literal strings 'yes'/'no' (resolved via a market lookup) or a raw outcome token ID.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Unix timestamp (ms) — fetch a historical snapshot at or before this time, or the start of a range when combined with `until` (hosted API only).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Unix timestamp (ms) — end of a historical range. When combined with `since`, returns an array of reconstructed L2 OrderBook snapshots between `since` and `until` (hosted API only).

get.description: Fetch the order book (bids/asks) for a specific outcome. Supports live and historical queries. For historical data, pass `since` to get a single snapshot, or `since` + `until` to get an array of fully reconstructed L2 books from the archive. Range queries return up to `limit` snapshots (default 100, max 1000).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Keyword search across matched market titles.

parameters.name: market
parameters.description: Pass a UnifiedMarket directly instead of marketId/slug/url.

parameters.name: marketId
parameters.description: Lookup a specific market by ID. Omit for browse mode.

parameters.name: minDifference
parameters.description: Minimum price difference between venues. Browse mode only.

get.description: Find the same or related market on other venues. Two modes: **Lookup mode** (marketId/slug/url provided): Given a market on one venue, discover semantically equivalent markets across every other venue PMXT ingests. **Browse mode** (no identifier): Returns all matched market pairs from the catalog. Supports query, category, minDifference, and sort params for filtering.

x-codeSamples.label: Router
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

# API key required — queries the cross-venue catalog

    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
)
result = exchange.fetch_market_matches(market_id="12345")

get.description:
Stream live ticker updates for a symbol via WebSocket.

**Transport:** WebSocket

| Environment | URL |
|---|---|
| Hosted API | `wss://api.pmxt.dev/ws?apiKey=YOUR_KEY` |

**Subscribe:**
```json
{ "id": "btc-stream", "action": "subscribe", "method": "watchTicker", "args": ["BTC/USDT"], "feed": "binance" }
```

**Server response:**
```json
{ "event": "data", "id": "btc-stream", "method": "watchTicker", "symbol": "BTC/USDT", "source": "binance", "data": { "symbol": "BTC/USDT", "last": 76949.16, "timestamp": 1716148800000, ... } }
```

**Unsubscribe:**
```json
{ "id": "btc-stream", "action": "unsubscribe" }
```

Currently supports Binance feeds only. Tickers stream as they arrive from the Binance trade relay.

    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",

ExchangeParam.name: exchange
ExchangeParam.description: The prediction market exchange to target.

ErrorDetail.description: Structured error envelope returned inside `BaseResponse.error` and `ErrorResponse.error`. Hosted-mode endpoints populate `code`, `retryable`, and optionally `exchange` / `detail`; legacy local-mode endpoints may still return only `message`.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. Hosted-mode errors use the `HostedTradingError` family (e.g. `INSUFFICIENT_ESCROW_BALANCE`, `BUILT_ORDER_EXPIRED`); pre-hosted local errors use the legacy family (e.g. `BAD_REQUEST`, `NOT_FOUND`).

retryable.description: Hint for clients: when `true`, the same request may succeed on retry (e.g. transient network or rate-limit conditions); when `false`, the caller should not retry without modifying the request.

exchange.description: Venue the error originated from, when known (e.g. 'polymarket', 'kalshi').

detail.description: Free-form hosted-mode detail blob. Shape depends on `code` — e.g. for `INSUFFICIENT_ESCROW_BALANCE` it may include `{ requested, available }`; for `ORDER_SIZE_TOO_SMALL` it may include `{ min }`; for `BUILT_ORDER_EXPIRED` it may include `{ expiry }`.

ExchangeOptions.description:
Constructor-level options for venue clients (Polymarket, Kalshi, Opinion, etc.).
Hosted mode is the default when pmxtApiKey is set; otherwise the SDK runs against
a local sidecar with venue credentials.

pmxtApiKey.description: PMXT customer API key. When set, the SDK routes to api.pmxt.dev (catalog) and trade.pmxt.dev (trading). Get one at pmxt.dev/dashboard.

walletAddress.description: EVM wallet address for hosted reads/writes. Required for endpoints that operate on a wallet (balances, positions, trades, open orders).

signer.description: Optional pre-built signer for hosted writes. If absent and privateKey is set, the SDK auto-wraps privateKey into a signer.

privateKey.description: Private key. In hosted mode, used to derive an EIP-712 signer for writes (wraps into EthAccountSigner/EthersSigner). In self-hosted mode, used as the venue credential directly.

baseUrl.description: Explicit base URL override. When unset, the SDK uses api.pmxt.dev when pmxtApiKey is set, or the local sidecar otherwise.

apiKey.description: Venue-side API key (e.g. Polymarket CLOB key). Only relevant for self-hosted mode.

autoStartServer.description: Auto-start the local sidecar when running self-hosted. Defaults to true when no pmxtApiKey is set, false when hosted.

BaseRequest.description: Base request structure with optional credentials

marketId.description: The unique identifier for this market

title.description: The market title (e.g., "Will BTC close above $100k on Dec 31?").

description.description: Long-form market description or resolution criteria.

outcomes.description: The possible outcomes for this market.

resolutionDate.description: When the market is scheduled to resolve. Optional because some venues do not publish a cutoff for every market (e.g. Opinion categorical children) — emit `undefined` rather than coercing to epoch.

volume24h.description: Trading volume over the past 24 hours (USD).

url.description: Canonical URL to view the market on the venue.

image.description: Optional image URL for the market.

category.description: Optional category label. Venue-defined — common values include "Sports", "Politics", "Crypto", "Economics", "Science", "Culture". Polymarket uses finer-grained categories like "Bitcoin", "Soccer", "Economic Policy"; Kalshi uses broader ones like "Sports" or "Mentions".

tags.description: Optional list of tags. More granular than category — e.g. ["Crypto", "Crypto Prices", "Bitcoin"] or ["Politics", "Elections", "Trump"]. Tags vary by venue: Polymarket markets carry several, Kalshi typically one.

contractAddress.description: On-chain contract / condition identifier where applicable (Polymarket conditionId, etc.).

sourceMetadata.description: Raw venue-specific metadata not captured by first-class fields (e.g. Kalshi series_ticker / series_title from the parent event, Polymarket series). Passed through verbatim so downstream consumers can recover anything the unified shape omits. Each venue populates what it has.

sourceExchange.description: The exchange/venue this market originates from (e.g. 'polymarket', 'kalshi'). Populated by the Router.

yes.description: Convenience accessor for the YES outcome on a binary market.

no.description: Convenience accessor for the NO outcome on a binary market.

up.description: Convenience accessor for the UP outcome on a binary market.

down.description: Convenience accessor for the DOWN outcome on a binary market.

outcomeId.description: Outcome ID for trading operations (CLOB Token ID for Polymarket, Market Ticker for Kalshi)

marketId.description: The market this outcome belongs to (set automatically when outcomes are built)

priceChange24h.description: Change in price over the past 24 hours, as an absolute probability delta.

UnifiedEvent.description: A grouped collection of related markets (e.g., "Who will be Fed Chair?" contains multiple candidate markets).

id.description: The unique identifier for this event.

title.description: The event title (e.g., "Who will be Fed Chair?").

volume24h.description: Trading volume over the past 24 hours (USD).

volume.description: Total / Lifetime volume (sum across markets; undefined if no market provides it)

url.description: Canonical URL to view the event on the venue.

image.description: Optional image URL for the event.

category.description: Optional category label. Venue-defined — common values include "Sports", "Politics", "Crypto", "Economics", "Science", "Culture". Polymarket uses finer-grained categories like "Bitcoin", "Soccer", "Economic Policy"; Kalshi uses broader ones like "Sports" or "Mentions".

tags.description: Optional list of tags. More granular than category — e.g. ["Sports", "FIFA World Cup", "2026 FIFA World Cup"] or ["Politics", "Geopolitics", "Middle East"]. Tags vary by venue: Polymarket markets carry several, Kalshi typically one.

sourceMetadata.description: Raw venue-specific metadata not captured by first-class fields (e.g. Kalshi series_ticker / series_title, Polymarket series). Passed through verbatim so downstream consumers can recover anything the unified shape omits. Each venue populates what it has.

sourceExchange.description: The exchange/venue this event originates from (e.g. 'polymarket', 'kalshi'). Populated by the Router.

UnifiedSeries.description: A recurring grouping of events on a venue — the fourth tier above Event -> Market -> Outcome. Examples: Kalshi `KXATPMATCH` (every ATP tennis match), Polymarket `wta` (every WTA match), Opinion's daily `collection`. Series only exists where the venue exposes a recurring-event concept; venues without one return an empty array from `fetchSeries`.

id.description: Stable venue-native series identifier (e.g. "KXATPMATCH" on Kalshi, "atp" on Polymarket Gamma, numeric Gamma id).

events.description: Child events. Populated when fetched by id; the list form usually omits this to keep payloads small.

url.description: Canonical venue URL for the series.

sourceExchange.description: The exchange this series originates from. Populated by the Router.

sourceMetadata.description: Raw venue-specific fields not promoted to first-class columns.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds marking the start of the candle.

open.description: Opening price for the interval (probability between 0.0 and 1.0).

high.description: Highest price during the interval (probability between 0.0 and 1.0).

low.description: Lowest price during the interval (probability between 0.0 and 1.0).

close.description: Closing price for the interval (probability between 0.0 and 1.0).

bids.description: Order book bid levels, sorted by price descending.

asks.description: Order book ask levels, sorted by price ascending.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the snapshot was taken.

datetime.description: ISO 8601 datetime string of the snapshot (CCXT-compatible).

id.description: The unique identifier for this trade.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the trade executed.

amount.description: Size of the trade in contracts/shares.

side.description: Trade side from the taker's perspective.

outcomeId.description: The outcome this trade is for (if known).

id.description: The unique identifier for this trade.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the trade executed.

amount.description: Size of the trade in contracts/shares.

side.description: Trade side from the taker's perspective.

outcomeId.description: The outcome this trade is for (if known).

orderId.description: The order that produced this trade, if known.

txHash.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

chain.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

blockNumber.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

marketId.description: The market this order was placed on.

outcomeId.description: The outcome this order was placed on.

type.description: Order type: market (execute immediately) or limit (resting at a price).

filled.description: Amount filled (USDC cost for buys, shares for sells)

filledShares.description: Amount filled in shares/contracts (if different from USDC-denominated `filled`).

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the order was created.

fee.description: Fee paid for this order, if known.

feeRateBps.description: Fee rate in basis points applied to this order (e.g. 100 = 1%).

txHash.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

chain.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

blockNumber.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement; null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

Position.description: A current position in a market. In hosted mode, `outcomeLabel`, `entryPrice`, `currentPrice` and `unrealizedPnL` may be null when the server cannot derive them (e.g. `with_mtm=false` or no fill history). Venue-direct callers continue to populate every field.

marketId.description: The market this position is held in.

outcomeId.description: The outcome this position is held in.

outcomeLabel.description: Human-readable label for the outcome held. Optional in hosted mode.

size.description: Positive for long, negative for short

entryPrice.description: Average entry price for the position (probability between 0.0 and 1.0). Optional in hosted mode when no fill history is available.

currentPrice.description: Current mark price for the position (probability between 0.0 and 1.0). Optional in hosted mode when mark-to-market data is unavailable.

currentValue.description: Current market value of the position (size * currentPrice). Null when currentPrice is unavailable.

unrealizedPnL.description: Unrealized profit or loss at the current price (USD). Optional in hosted mode when mark-to-market data is unavailable.

realizedPnL.description: Realized profit or loss booked so far (USD).

txHash.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement (from the last fill); null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

chain.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement (from the last fill); null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

blockNumber.description: Populated in hosted mode after on-chain settlement (from the last fill); null for local-mode and for non-on-chain venues.

total.description: Total balance including funds locked in open orders.

available.description: Balance available to trade (excludes locked funds).

venue.description: Hosted-mode: which venue this balance belongs to in a multi-venue response. Null when the balance is venue-agnostic.

total.description: Total number of markets in the snapshot

nextCursor.description: Cursor to pass to the next call, or undefined if this is the last page

total.description: Total number of events in the snapshot

nextCursor.description: Cursor to pass to the next call, or undefined if this is the last page

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

offset.description: Pagination offset — number of results to skip

status.description: Filter by market status (default: 'active', 'inactive' and 'closed' are interchangeable)

outcomeId.description: Reverse lookup -- find market containing this outcome

eventId.description: Find markets belonging to an event

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

cursor.description: Opaque venue pagination cursor, where supported.

offset.description: Pagination offset — number of results to skip

status.description: Filter by event status (default: 'active', 'inactive' and 'closed' are interchangeable)

series.description: Filter events by their parent series. Accepts the venue-native series id / ticker / slug (e.g. Kalshi `"KXATPMATCH"`, Polymarket `"wta"`). Passed through to the vendor where supported, otherwise applied to `sourceMetadata` after fetch.

filter.description: Optional client-side filter applied after fetching

category.description: Filter by category. Each event belongs to a venue-assigned category such as "Sports", "Politics", "Crypto", "Bitcoin", "Soccer", "Economic Policy" (Polymarket) or "Sports", "Mentions" (Kalshi).

tags.description: Filter by tags. Returns events matching ANY of the provided tags. Tags are more specific than categories -- for example a "Politics" event might carry tags ["Politics", "Geopolitics", "Middle East", "Iran"]. Common tags include "Crypto", "Elections", "Fed Rates", "FIFA World Cup", "Trump".

HistoryFilterParams.description: Deprecated - use OHLCVParams or TradesParams instead. Resolution is optional for backward compatibility.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return
Maximum number of results to return

side.description: Outcome side: 'yes' or 'no'. Required for exchanges like Limitless where the API returns a single orderbook per market.

outcome.description: Outcome alias: 'yes' or 'no', or an outcome token ID. When set, the first argument is treated as a market ID and this value selects which outcome's order book to fetch. Accepts the literal strings 'yes'/'no' (resolved via a market lookup) or a raw outcome token ID.

since.description: Unix timestamp (ms) — fetch a historical snapshot at or before this time, or the start of a range when combined with `until` (hosted API only).

until.description: Unix timestamp (ms) — end of a historical range. When combined with `since`, returns an array of reconstructed L2 OrderBook snapshots between `since` and `until` (hosted API only).

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (max {@link MAX_TRADES_LIMIT})

type.description: Order type: market (execute immediately) or limit (resting at a price).

amount.description: Size of the order in contracts/shares.

negRisk.description: Optional override to skip neg-risk lookup (Polymarket)

onBehalfOf.description: Limitless delegated signing: profile ID to trade on behalf of

exchange.description: The exchange name this order was built for.

params.description: The original params used to build this order.

signedOrder.description: For CLOB exchanges (Polymarket): the EIP-712 signed order ready to POST to the exchange's order endpoint.

tx.description: For on-chain AMM exchanges: the EVM transaction payload. Reserved for future exchanges; no current exchange populates this.

raw.description: The raw, exchange-native payload. Always present.

expiry.description: Unix epoch (ms) when this built order expires server-side. Submitting after expiry returns BUILT_ORDER_EXPIRED.

until.description: Only return records before this date

funderAddress.description: The address funding the trades (defaults to signer address)
